data.body:
## Context

DSO skill authors cannot measure whether their changes improve or degrade skill quality. The project has 57 structural tests (grep/pattern-based) and a declarative eval framework, but no tests that exercise skills with real LLM invocation and grade the output. Cross-cutting regressions — where a hook, shared library, or CLAUDE.md change silently breaks multiple skills — go undetected until a developer hits the problem manually. Model drift from upstream Claude updates is similarly invisible. This epic adds a promptfoo-based eval framework with two tiers: change-gated evals at commit time for direct regressions, and a daily full-suite run that auto-creates a P0 bug ticket on failure so regressions surface loudly in the sprint workflow.

## Success Criteria

1. Each skill can define evals in a co-located evals/ directory containing a promptfooconfig.yaml with test cases using promptfoo's native llm-rubric assertion (quality grading) and skill-used assertion (trigger routing verification) against promptfoo's anthropic:claude-agent-sdk provider — all three are existing, documented promptfoo features.
2. An orchestrator script (run-skill-evals.sh) operates in two modes: (a) Tier 1 — accepts a list of changed skill directory paths, finds their evals/promptfooconfig.yaml files, runs promptfoo eval, and exits non-zero on any failure; (b) Tier 2 — discovers all evals/promptfooconfig.yaml files under plugins/dso/skills/, runs the full suite, and outputs promptfoo's native JSON results (the --output format with success, score, and testIdx fields per result).
3. record-test-status.sh maps staged skill files (detected via git diff --cached --name-only matching plugins/dso/skills/*/) to their parent skill directory and invokes the orchestrator for affected skills' evals as part of the commit-time test gate.
4. A daily CI workflow (GitHub Actions cron) runs the Tier 2 orchestrator. On any failure, it creates a P0 bug ticket titled "EVAL REGRESSION: <date> — <N> skills failing" with failing eval details — or, if an open P0 bug with the "EVAL REGRESSION:" title prefix already exists, appends a comment with the new failure details instead of creating a duplicate.
5. sprint-list-epics.sh queries for open P0 bugs and displays them above the epic list when any exist, ensuring eval regressions are visible before developers select work.
6. Quality evals use Haiku as the default grader model (set explicitly via promptfoo's defaultTest.options.provider), with a per-eval config override to Sonnet for rubrics requiring deeper reasoning.
7. At least 3 representative skills ship with evals (one simple, one complex, one with trigger routing tests), and a documented smoke test demonstrates the framework detecting an intentionally-introduced regression.

## Dependencies

- promptfoo (new npm dev dependency — requires user approval per DEPENDENCY-GUIDANCE.md)

## Constraints

- record-test-status.sh is a protected safeguard file (CLAUDE.md rule 20) — modifications require explicit user approval
- Daily CI ticket creation requires the CI environment to have write access to the tickets orphan branch (same access pattern as ticket-lifecycle.yml)
- The anthropic:claude-agent-sdk provider is pre-1.0 (^0.2.42) — pin the version to avoid surprise breakage

## Approach

Per-skill promptfoo configs with a thin orchestrator. Two tiers: Tier 1 integrates into record-test-status.sh for change-gated evals at commit time; Tier 2 runs daily in CI with auto-P0 ticket creation on failure (dedupe by "EVAL REGRESSION:" title prefix). Uses promptfoo's anthropic:claude-agent-sdk provider to sidestep the claude -p skill trigger limitation (anthropics/claude-code#36570).
